brief: Knowledge graph reasoning is the fastest-moving category this week — 3 papers with direct product applications.
Speech disentanglement crossed from pure research into deployable SDK territory — watch for Hanverse applications.
Multimodal memory architectures are converging with RAG — a new 'M-RAG' pattern is emerging.
Long-context LLM inference (< 1s at 128k) enables a new class of 'reading-level' apps.
Sparse attention with learnable patterns reduces 70% FLOPs — ideal for on-device AI.

top_cards.title: New Technologies
top_cards.label: emerged this week
top_cards.detail: Sparse Flow Attention, GraphKV, M-RAG, SpeechFlow Disentanglement, Neural Memory Network, Adaptive Token Routing, CrossModal Embeddings 2.0, Recursive Feedback Loops, Hierarchical KV Cache, Differentiable Ranking, Learned Sparse Retrieval, Embedding Compression via Product Quantization

top_cards.title: Emerging Trends
top_cards.label: active trend clusters
top_cards.detail: Knowledge Graph for LLM, Speech Disentanglement, Multimodal Memory, Sparse Attention, Long Context Inference

top_cards.title: Highest Potential
top_cards.label: avg opportunity score
top_cards.detail: Knowledge River (Hanverse), Grammar Flow Visualization, Shadowing Correction Engine, Adaptive Review Scheduler, Stroke Order Intelligence

top_cards.title: Biggest Surprise
top_cards.label: disentanglement is production-ready
top_cards.detail: Speech disentanglement research has matured faster than expected. What was theoretical 6 months ago now has open-source implementations. Direct application: real-time shadowing correction for language learners.

top_cards.title: Most Practical
top_cards.label: can ship in ~2 weeks
top_cards.detail: Multimodal RAG combines text + image + audio retrieval in a single pipeline. Can be implemented as a NoteGPT-style feature for existing apps. Low engineering risk, high user value.

top_cards.title: Most Innovative
top_cards.label: novel KV cache architecture
top_cards.detail: Replaces linear KV cache with a directed graph structure, enabling sub-linear memory growth during long context inference. Potentially transformative for on-device LLMs.

papers.title: Sparse Flow Attention: Differentiable Structured Pruning for Long-Context LLMs
papers.summary: Introduces a learnable sparse attention pattern that reduces FLOPs by 70% while maintaining 98% of full-attention quality on long-context benchmarks. Key insight: attention flow can be modeled as a weighted directed graph, and pruning can be learned end-to-end via a Gumbel-sigmoid relaxation.
product_potential: On-device LLM inference for mobile apps
Real-time document analysis at 10x lower cost
Edge AI chatbots with full conversation history
hanverse_applications: Real-time stroke-order analysis without server costs
On-device grammar correction for offline learning

papers.title: GraphKV: Hierarchical Key-Value Cache with Sub-Linear Memory Growth
papers.summary: Proposes a hierarchical KV cache structured as a directed acyclic graph. Frequently accessed tokens are cached at higher levels, enabling O(log n) memory growth for n-token contexts. Achieves 4x compression on 128k context without quality loss.
product_potential: Serverless LLM inference at 1/4 the cost
Mobile apps with full conversation memory
hanverse_applications: Learning sessions that remember every interaction
Student progress tracking across months of study

papers.title: M-RAG: Multimodal Retrieval Augmented Generation with Unified Embedding Space
papers.summary: Extends RAG to multiple modalities (text, image, audio, video) using a unified embedding space learned via contrastive objectives. Achieves 92% retrieval accuracy across modalities. Includes a novel 'cross-modal routing' mechanism.
product_potential: AI notebook that combines notes, sketches, and voice memos
Educational apps that search across textbooks, videos, and diagrams
Medical diagnosis support across imaging and clinical notes
hanverse_applications: Search across flashcards, audio recordings, and handwritten notes
Cross-modal review: hear a word → see its character → read example sentence

papers.title: SpeechFlow Disentanglement: Separating Content, Prosody, and Speaker Identity Without Supervision
papers.summary: Achieves unsupervised disentanglement of speech into content, prosody, and speaker identity using a flow-based generative model. Enables prosody transfer, content-preserving voice conversion, and accent manipulation without parallel data.
product_potential: Language learning with perfect native pronunciation transfer
Audiobook narration with user-preferred voice styles
hanverse_applications: Shadowing correction: compare user pronunciation to native, isolate errors
Prosody training: visualize and correct tone and intonation
Voice cloning for personalized learning companions

papers.title: Neural Memory Network: Differentiable Experience Replay for Continual Learning in LLMs
papers.summary: Introduces a neural memory module that stores and retrieves past experiences (training examples, user interactions) using differentiable addressing. Enables LLMs to learn from deployment without catastrophic forgetting. Memory grows sub-linearly via clustering.
product_potential: Personalized AI that remembers user preferences over time
Educational AI that adapts to each student's learning history
Customer support AI that improves from every interaction
hanverse_applications: Personalized learning model that tracks every user's weak points
AI tutor that remembers which characters the student always confuses

product_opportunities.idea: Knowledge River — visualize language learning as a flowing river where words, grammar rules, and characters form a directed graph. Show where knowledge 'leaks' (weak areas) as eroded riverbanks.
Shadowing Correction Engine — learner speaks, AI disentangles content from pronunciation, highlights exact phonemes that need correction, shows native speaker's prosody as a visual target.
Cross-Modal Flashcard Generator — upload a PDF, video, or audio. AI automatically generates flashcards with text, images from the material, and audio pronunciation. Search across all modalities.
Offline AI Tutor — full conversation history, stroke memory, grammar corrections — all running on-device. No server costs, works on planes and in remote areas.
